steps.title: Find the common factor
steps.content: Each term in $7x^5-4x^4+9x^3$ has a factor of $x^3$. The coefficients $7$, $4$, and $9$ have no common factor greater than $1$, so the common factor to remove is $x^3$.

steps.title: Divide each term
steps.content: After taking out $x^3$, divide every term by that factor. The three remaining terms are $7x^2$, $-4x$, and $9$, in the same order as the original expression.

steps.title: Write the factored expression
steps.content: Put the remaining terms inside parentheses: $$ 7x^5-4x^4+9x^3=x^3(7x^2-4x+9) $$ This matches the equivalent expression in the choices. The answer is B.
